title: loop.meta
description: A loop's identity and relation — the file that carries everything the loop directory name deliberately omits. Holds the parent/child relation, spawn reason, slug, title, owner node, scope, and return contract. Field names and enum values are locked byte-for-byte per references/recursive_loops.md §6. status reuses the 15 canonical node statuses from references/state_model.md; type is the 2-value root_loop | child_loop enum.

loop_id.description: The immutable loop id (L001, L001.02, L001.02.01). Primary key.

slug.description: The kebab-case slug from the directory name.

type.description: One of root_loop | child_loop. root_loop for a top-level loop; child_loop for any materialized child at any depth.

parent.description: The parent relation. null (or absent) only for a root_loop. For a child_loop, an object naming the parent loop, its relative path, the owning node, and the spawn reason.

path.description: Path to the parent loop directory, relative to this child's own directory (e.g. ../..).

parent_node_id.description: The id of the node in the parent's loop.plan.yaml that spawned this child loop (the owning node).

spawn_reason.description: Why this child loop was materialized (which Admission Gate criterion held).

root.description: The top-of-tree relation pointing at the root loop of this tree. For a root_loop, root points at itself.

path.description: Path to the root loop directory, relative to this loop's own directory.

status.description: Identity-level status. One of the 15 canonical node statuses. Terminal: completed, cancelled, deprecated.

created_at.description: When the loop directory was created (ISO-8601 date-time).

created_by.description: Who/what created the loop (agent, subagent, or user identifier).

depth.description: Recursion depth. 0 for a top-level (root_loop); 1 for its direct children; 2 for grandchildren; and so on.

scope.description: The loop's boundary: what is in and out of scope. Each list may be empty ([]).

in.description: What is in scope for this loop. May be empty ([]).

out.description: What is explicitly out of scope for this loop. May be empty ([]).

return_contract.description: How this loop returns to its parent. For a root_loop, closeout_file still points at its own closeout.md and parent_updates may be empty ([]).

closeout_file.description: Path (relative to this loop's directory) to the closeout document. Conventionally closeout.md.

required_outputs.description: The artifact paths this loop MUST produce before it may close out. May be empty ([]) only for a loop that produces no artifacts (rare).

parent_updates.description: The updates the parent must apply once this loop closes. May be empty ([]) for a root_loop.
